title: SMTP Email Flow — Mail Transfer Architecture
examTip: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol, Port 25/587) is used for SENDING email from client to server and between mail servers. POP3 (Port 110) and IMAP (Port 143) are used for RECEIVING email. SMTP communication uses plain-text commands: HELO/EHLO, MAIL FROM, RCPT TO, DATA, QUIT. The mail flow path is: Sender's Mail Client → Sender's MTA (SMTP) → DNS lookup (MX record) → Recipient's MTA (SMTP) → Recipient's Mailbox → Read via POP3/IMAP.

blocks.label: SMTP Email Flow — From Sender to Recipient
→ DATA (body follows) ... Subject: ... . (period on single line = end of message)
Recipient Reads Mail using POP3 (port 110) or IMAP (port 143)
blocks.note: POP3 = downloads & deletes from server. IMAP = syncs, keeps on server (multi-device).

theory:
SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) is the standard protocol for sending email across the internet. It operates over TCP and uses a simple command-response model.

Key Ports:
- Port 25: SMTP relay between mail servers (MTA to MTA). Often blocked by ISPs to prevent spam.
- Port 587: SMTP submission from mail client to its own mail server (authenticated).
- Port 465: SMTPS — SMTP over SSL/TLS.
- Port 110: POP3 — Post Office Protocol (download and optionally delete from server).
- Port 143: IMAP — Internet Message Access Protocol (sync, keep on server, multi-device).

MTA, MDA, MUA:
- MUA (Mail User Agent): Email client used by the sender/recipient (Outlook, Gmail web, Apple Mail).
- MTA (Mail Transfer Agent): Mail server that routes/relays email between domains (Postfix, Sendmail, Gmail's servers).
- MDA (Mail Delivery Agent): Delivers mail from MTA to the recipient's mailbox on disk.

SMTP Command Summary:
- EHLO/HELO: Greeting — client identifies itself.
- MAIL FROM: <sender>: Specifies envelope sender.
- RCPT TO: <recipient>: Specifies recipient (can be repeated for multiple recipients).
- DATA: Begins message body. Body terminated by a single period (.) on its own line.
- QUIT: Closes the connection.

Response codes (3-digit): 2xx = Success, 4xx = Temporary failure (retry), 5xx = Permanent failure.
keyPoints: SMTP (port 25/587) = sending email. POP3 (port 110) and IMAP (port 143) = receiving email.
Command flow: EHLO → MAIL FROM → RCPT TO → DATA → QUIT.
DNS MX (Mail Exchange) records identify the mail server for a domain.
Response codes: 2xx = OK, 4xx = retry later, 5xx = permanent error.
POP3 downloads mail and can delete from server. IMAP syncs and keeps mail on server (multi-device friendly).
SMTP relay uses port 25 (MTA↔MTA); client submission uses port 587 with authentication.
useCases: Understanding email delivery path and troubleshooting mail delivery failures.
Configuring mail servers (Postfix, Exchange, Gmail SMTP relay) in system administration.
Exam questions on port numbers, SMTP commands, and MX record resolution.
